top_signals.title: Japan’s Katayama Confirms Talks With Bessent on FX Agreement
top_signals.summary: Japan’s Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said she had talks over phone with US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, a move that only gave the yen a short-lived boost as it hovers near the weakest level in four decades.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: ‘A Lot of Leeway’: SpaceX’s High-Grade Debt Brings Out Skeptics
top_signals.summary: When Moody’s Ratings first evaluated Nvidia Corp. almost a decade ago, it settled on a Baa1 investment-grade rating, taking comfort in its relatively light debt load and more than $1 billion of free cash flow after 16 years as a public company.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Ether’s biggest corporate holders back new Ethereum research hub
top_signals.why_it_matters: Digital asset volatility and institutional crypto positioning changes.

top_signals.title: China keeps lending benchmark LPRs unchanged for 13th month in June - Reuters
top_signals.summary: China keeps lending benchmark LPRs unchanged for 13th month in June  Reuters
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: SpaceX readies its $20 billion bond debut to fund AI ambition
top_signals.summary: Much of what the company offers investors now is hope about future profitability, but for bond buyers, that may be a harder sale.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Dangerous ‘heat stress’ has surged worldwide, study shows
top_signals.summary: Most of the world has seen increases in the number of tropical nights, when the "feels-like" temperature does not get below 20 C, researchers found.

top_signals.title: EU nations want reduced protection for bears
top_signals.summary: Bears killed 18 people and seriously injured more than 200 in the two countries over the past five years.
